title: 10 best herbal essential oils for frozen shoulder
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for frozen shoulder are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate the pain and stiffness associated with this condition. They work by reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and relaxing tense muscles. The best herbal essential oils for frozen shoulder include Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Eucalyptus globulus, Capsicum annuum, Cinnamomum verum, Rosmarinus officinalis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Lavandula angustifolia, Cinnamomum zeylanicum, and Artemisia absinthium. If left untreated, frozen shoulder can significantly impact daily life, causing persistent pain, limited mobility, and emotional distress, making everyday activities a chore and affecting overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The oil also contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that blocks the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that promote inflammation. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to have analgesic and antioxidant properties, helping to relieve stiffness and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder. By applying topically, this oil can help alleviate symptoms and promote healing in the affected joint.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal licorice essential oil has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for improved mobility and reduced stiffness.

Analgesic: The analgesic properties of herbal licorice essential oil can help alleviate pain and discomfort in the affected joint, making it easier to perform daily activities and exercises to promote healing.

Antioxidant: Herbal licorice essential oil's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area, promoting a faster recovery from frozen shoulder and reducing the risk of chronic damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.

Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain a unique combination of phenolic compounds, including flavonoids and lignans, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief from frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality glycyrrhiza glabra roots and dry them in the shade for at least two weeks to minimize moisture content.
Crush 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle, then mix with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil.
Steam distill the mixture in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for four hours, collecting the essential oil that separates from the distillation liquid.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment, then store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.
Shake the bottle well before use and apply topically 2-3 times daily as needed, mixing with a carrier oil if necessary, for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and stiffness in the affected joints. The oil's ability to improve circulation also helps to loosen up any adhesions or scar tissue that may be contributing to the condition. Additionally, sage's natural warming properties can help increase blood flow and relax muscles, reducing tension and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage essential oil contains compounds like camphor and thujone, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, thus alleviating symptoms of frozen shoulder.

Pain-relieving: The presence of salvene, a sesquiterpene compound, in sage essential oil makes it an effective natural pain reliever, which can help alleviate the sharp pains associated with frozen shoulder.

Muscle relaxant: Sage essential oil contains compounds that have a relaxing effect on muscles and joints, which can help reduce stiffness and tension in the shoulders and surrounding areas, making it easier to move and stretch.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Barks: Sage barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that work together to relax muscles and improve joint mobility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality salvia officinalis leaves for steam distillation.
Prepare the plant material by cutting and cleaning the salvia officinalis.
Steam distill the prepared plant material using a commercial apparatus.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distilled plant material.
Filter and store the final salvia officinalis essential oil for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper essential oils helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The oil's analgesic qualities also help alleviate discomfort and stiffness, making it an effective natural remedy for frozen shoulder treatment. Additionally, bell pepper essential oil's antioxidant properties promote healthy tissue repair and regeneration, which aids in the healing process. By using this oil topically or through aromatherapy, individuals can experience relief from frozen shoulder symptoms without the need for pharmaceutical interventions.
remedy_properties: Capsaicin (found in Cinnamomum verum or Ceylon cinnamon oil): Has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Wintergreen (Gaultheria procumbens) oil: Contains methyl salicylate, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that may help reduce pain and inflammation in the affected shoulder joint.
Frankincense (Boswellia serrata) oil: Has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and stiffness in the shoulder joint, promoting healing and reducing pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Fruits: Bell pepper fruits, specifically the pulp and rind, have been traditionally used in herbal remedies for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum leaves and flowers on a sunny day for optimal oil quality.
Dry the harvested plant material using a dehydrator or air circulation method within 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material in a still with a 10:1 water to herb ratio at 100°c for 4 hours.
Filter and decant the resulting essential oil into dark glass bottles after cooling to room temperature.
Store the freshly produced capsicum annuum essential oil in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric essential oils helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The curcumin present in turmeric oil reduces inflammation and alleviates pain in the affected area, promoting relaxation and flexibility. Additionally, it increases blood flow to the joints, which helps to reduce stiffness and restore range of motion. By massaging turmeric essential oil into the skin or using it in a warm compress, individuals with frozen shoulder can experience relief from discomfort and improve overall mobility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric essential oil has potent anti-inflammatory compounds like curcuminoids that reduce swelling and pain in the joints and muscles, which can alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder.

Antioxidant property: Turmeric essential oil's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to chronic inflammation and oxidative stress, further reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected area.

Pain-relieving property: The essential oils present in turmeric, such as bisabolene and curcumin, have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes are the underground stems that contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.

Roots: Turmeric roots are rich in volatile oils that can be extracted to produce a medicinal essential oil that helps reduce inflammation and promotes relaxation, making them an effective remedy for managing frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh turmeric rhizomes and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the harvested turmeric rhizomes in a warm oven at 150°f for 24 hours.
Crush the dried turmeric rhizomes into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 30g of the turmeric powder with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining and bottling the curcuma longa essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower essential oils helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and stiffness in the affected joint. The oil's ability to soothe and calm the area also promotes relaxation, allowing for increased range of motion and reduced muscle spasms. Additionally, purple coneflower's analgesic properties work to dull the sharp pains associated with frozen shoulder, making it an effective natural remedy for managing this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Echinacea has been shown to reduce inflammation and pain in various conditions, which could help alleviate symptoms associated with frozen shoulder such as stiffness and limited mobility.
Immune system modulation: Echinacea's immunomodulatory effects may help regulate the immune response, potentially reducing the inflammatory response that contributes to frozen shoulder.

Antioxidant properties: Echinacea's antioxidant activity may help protect joints from oxidative damage caused by inflammation, which could contribute to the development and progression of frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Seeds: The seeds of the purple coneflower are rich in omega-3 fatty acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate joint pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers in sufficient quantity for distillation.
Cleanse the harvested flowers by gently shaking off excess dirt and debris.
Steam distill the cleaned flowers using a clevenger apparatus with water at 100 degrees celsius.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the aqueous phase and store it in dark glass bottles.
Allow the essential oil to mature for two weeks before use or further processing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree essential oils helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The oil contains a compound called casticin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating the stiffness and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder. Additionally, chaste tree oil's ability to soothe and calm the affected area can help reduce tension and promote healing, providing relief from chronic pain and stiffness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chaste tree essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for increased mobility and reduced stiffness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of chaste tree essential oil can help protect the body from further damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and frozen shoulder symptoms.

Muscle relaxant properties: Chaste tree essential oil's muscle relaxant properties can help alleviate tension and pain in the affected shoulder and surrounding muscles, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chaste tree contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.

Flowers: The flowers are rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which have been shown to possess analgesic and antispasmodic activities, helping to alleviate muscle cramps and stiffness in frozen shoulder patients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vitex agnus castus leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated and before noon when potency peaks.
Dry the leaves using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours or air dry them for 7 days.
Combine 100g of dried leaves with 500ml of carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, and filter out solids after 14 days using cheesecloth or coffee filters.
Transfer the infused oil to dark glass bottles and store in a cool place for up to 6 months with regular inspection.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with frozen shoulder because of its soothing and calming properties. It can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with this condition. Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory compounds work by decreasing prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and swelling. Additionally, the scent of lavender oil has a calming effect on the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing tension, which can exacerbate frozen shoulder symptoms. By applying lavender essential oils topically or inhaling its aroma, individuals with frozen shoulder may experience improved flexibility and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain associated with Frozen Shoulder, allowing for greater flexibility and range of motion.

Pain-relieving: The analgesic and anesthetic properties of lavender oil can provide relief from the sharp, stabbing pains that are common in Frozen Shoulder, helping to promote relaxation and reduce discomfort.
Antispasmodic: Lavender oil's antispasmodic properties may help alleviate muscle spasms and stiffness associated with Frozen Shoulder, allowing for greater mobility and reduced pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, lavender roots are used to create anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving essential oils that target frozen shoulder symptoms.
Buds: The young, delicate buds of the English lavender plant contain flavonoids and other beneficial compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing for frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers at dawn when fragrant for optimal quality and potency.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture.
Steam distill the dried lavender flowers using a copper alembic still or a commercial steam distillation unit for 4-6 hours.
Combine 20% lavender essential oil with 80% carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a dark glass bottle for storage.
Shake the blend well and apply topically to affected area 2-3 times daily for 8 weeks to alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose essential oils helps with frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and stiffness in the affected area. The oil's analgesic qualities also help to ease discomfort and promote relaxation, making it easier for people suffering from frozen shoulder to move and perform daily activities. Additionally, damask rose oil's antispasmodic properties can help to relax tense muscles and improve range of motion, allowing for a faster recovery from the condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Damask rose essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Pain-relieving property: The oil's analgesic (pain-relieving) properties can help alleviate the discomfort and stiffness caused by frozen shoulder, allowing for improved mobility and flexibility.
Antispasmodic property: Damask rose essential oil's antispasmodic properties can help relax and reduce muscle spasms in the affected areas, which is often a characteristic symptom of frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a rich source of bioactive compounds, the roots of the damask rose are valued for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.

Fruits: The fruits of the damask rose, also known as hips, are packed with vitamins and antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making them a valuable ingredient in essential oils for treating frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa damascena flowers in early morning for optimal oil content and aroma.
Dry the freshly harvested flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a small-scale steam distiller for 4-5 hours to extract oil.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a coffee filter into dark glass bottles and store in refrigerator.
Blend 10 drops of the rosa damascena essential oil with 2 tablespoons of carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with frozen shoulder because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. It has been traditionally used to reduce pain, stiffness, and swelling in joints, making it a valuable remedy for individuals experiencing frozen shoulder. The oil's analgesic and warming effects also help to increase circulation, which can aid in the reduction of joint stiffness and promote relaxation of tight muscles. Pain-relieving property: The analgesic and numbing properties of fennel essential oil can help alleviate chronic pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.

Muscle relaxant property: Fennel essential oil's ability to relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms can help loosen up tight and stiff muscles in the shoulder area, improving range of motion and reducing limited mobility.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile compounds, fennel roots have been traditionally used to relieve pain and reduce inflammation, making them an effective ingredient in essential oils for frozen shoulder.

Seeds: Containing a unique blend of flavonoids and terpenes, fennel seeds are often used in combination with other plant parts to create a synergistic effect that helps alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems from the wild or cultivated garden.
Dry the harvested material in a warm place for 7-10 days until it reaches a moisture content of 20%.
Steam distill the dried herb material using a traditional copper still or modern apparatus for 3 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a dark glass container and filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the final foeniculum vulgare essential oil in an amber-colored glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers in full bloom.
Dry the flowers using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill 100g of dried flowers in a clevenger apparatus for 4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol by decantation and filter through cheesecloth.
Store the resulting essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat frozen shoulder with herbal essential oils is to combine topical application with massage therapy. Blend 5ml of sweet marjoram oil, 3ml of eucalyptus oil, and 2ml of peppermint oil in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Massage the blend onto affected areas for 10-15 minutes, twice daily. This combination can help reduce pain, inflammation, and stiffness while promoting circulation and relaxation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to frozen shoulder that are treated with herbal essential oils are tendonitis, bursitis, and arthritis. These conditions involve inflammation and stiffness in joints and soft tissues, which can be alleviated by the anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of essential oils such as wintergreen, peppermint, and lavender. Additionally, frankincense and ginger oil can help reduce pain and inflammation, while rosemary and thyme oil may improve circulation and mobility.
